phrases.translation: I have spoken these things to you.
phrases.summary: The speaker says they have communicated these things directly to the listeners, indicating that the message is something already delivered and addressed to them.

phrases.translation: so that you may have peace in me.
phrases.summary: The purpose of what was spoken is stated: the listeners are meant to possess/receive peace by being in the speaker (i.e., grounded in him).

phrases.translation: In the world you have tribulation.
phrases.summary: The speaker acknowledges that the listeners will experience or are experiencing distress/tribulation in the world, describing their current or expected condition in worldly life.
The speaker urges the listeners to be courageous and encouraged despite the tribulation mentioned, giving an active instruction to maintain confidence.
The speaker declares that he has already achieved victory over the world, presenting himself as having overcome its influence or trials, which supports the encouragement to the listeners.

phrases.phrase: ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα ἐν ἐμοὶ εἰρήνην ἔχητε.
phrases.translation: I have spoken these things to you so that you may have peace in me.
phrases.summary: The speaker frames the message they delivered as having the goal of enabling the listeners to hold/possess peace in him.

phrases.phrase: ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ θλῖψιν ἔχετε· ἀλλὰ θαρσεῖτε, ἐγὼ νενίκηκα τὸν κόσμον.
phrases.translation: In the world you have tribulation; but take heart, I have overcome the world.
phrases.summary: The speaker contrasts the listeners’ tribulation in the world with a command to be encouraged, grounded in his claim that he has already defeated/overcome the world.
